tldr: Move from Sift's ML trust-and-safety platform to Kount's Mastercard-backed fraud and identity trust stack while keeping device fingerprinting, scoring, and chargeback workflows intact.
why_switch: Kount publishes a lower entry price point and more transparent pricing than Sift
Mastercard ownership gives Kount tighter card-network signal and chargeback data
Shared merchant network adds cross-merchant fraud signal Sift relies more on per-customer ML
Better fit if you already lean into card-payment fraud rather than full account-lifecycle abuse
Kount has a free tier for evaluation; Sift requires sales contact
what_to_export: List of integrated event types (login, signup, transaction, content) and their schemas
Custom rules, score thresholds, and decision logic configured in Sift Console
Historical event data and decisions for model warm-up (request via support if needed)
Chargeback feedback and labeled fraud outcomes
export_method: Sift exposes data and configuration primarily through its console and APIs. Use the Sift Events / Decisions APIs to pull historical event and decision data, and export rules, lists, and workflow definitions from the console. For bulk historical data or labeled outcomes, contact Sift support — there is no single one-click export, so plan a scripted pull. Consult the official Sift docs for current endpoint paths and rate limits.
import_method: Kount integration is typically driven by their JavaScript Data Collector (device fingerprint) plus server-side scoring API calls during transactions. Re-instrument your client and server with Kount SDKs, recreate equivalent rules and lists in the Kount control panel, and replay a sample of historical events through their API to validate scores before flipping production traffic. Use a shadow-mode period where both systems score in parallel.

concept_mapping.note: Re-map event payloads; field names differ — verify in current Kount docs.
Replace tag on every relevant page; tokens flow with the transaction call.
Manual recreation; logic differs in expression syntax.
Score scales and meanings differ — recalibrate thresholds with parallel runs.
Export as CSV from Sift, import into Kount.
Continue feeding outcomes to the new system to keep models tuned.

gotchas: Score calibration is not 1:1 — run shadow mode for at least one full billing cycle before changing decision automation
Device fingerprint identities do not transfer; trust history rebuilds from zero on the new platform
Webhook signatures and retry semantics differ — re-validate downstream consumers
PCI / data-residency review may be required when re-routing card data through a new vendor
Cancel Sift only after Kount is in full enforcement mode and chargeback feedback loop is wired

email.subject: Migrating from Sift to Kount — plan and timeline
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move our fraud and risk stack from Sift to Kount. Drivers: more transparent pricing, Mastercard-backed card-network signal, and a shared merchant network that fits our payment-fraud profile.

Plan:
1) Export rules, lists, decisions, and historical events from Sift via their APIs and console.
2) Stand up Kount in shadow mode — Data Collector on the client, scoring API server-side — running in parallel with Sift.
3) Recreate rules, thresholds, and workflows in Kount; calibrate score thresholds against the shadow data.
4) Cut over decision automation once shadow metrics look healthy for a full chargeback cycle.
5) Wire chargeback feedback into Kount, then decommission Sift.

Estimated effort: 3-8 weeks depending on rule complexity. I'll share a more detailed runbook before we start.

Thanks,
